summary: Plan-mode PR inventory classification only. No shared canonical was selected because the job is explicitly not a dedupe cluster. #93240 is routed to central security handling per deterministic validation of the security-sensitive target. The remaining listed refs are open pull requests in the preflight artifact; no closure, merge, or fix action is planned because merge/fix are blocked, low-signal PR closeout is disabled, checks were unavailable due GraphQL rate limiting, and several PRs require proof, author follow-up, or maintainer technical judgment.

actions.reason: The validator requires #93240 to be treated as security-sensitive. Under the job and worker security boundary, the safe non-mutating plan-mode action is route_security for this exact PR, with no close, merge, label, comment, or fix action.

This is maintainer-owned/broad taxonomy content with proof and automation-risk questions. The job explicitly requires human review for maintainer signal and technical correctness judgment, so keep it out of automated close/merge decisions.

The PR may be a valid narrow fix, but proof is still labeled insufficient and validation includes a failing test. Technical correctness and compatibility need maintainer review before any closure or merge path.

This appears to be a plausible localized bug fix, but the linked issue is not hydrated as a primary item, proof is still requested, comments were not hydrated, and correctness depends on Windows encoding behavior. Human review is required for the unresolved technical decision.

This may be a useful hardening/behavior PR, but the artifact does not prove current behavior, checks are unavailable, and the PR remains labeled needs proof. Human technical judgment is needed before any mutating path.
